free_text: Observation of 15N + alpha resonant structures in 19F
using the thick target in inverse kinematics scattering
method
Extreme Light Infrastructure-Nuclear
Physics (ELI-NP) , Magurele, Romania
Tandem accelerator at the
Laboratori Nazionali del Sud in Catania
CT2000 2-m-diameter scattering chamber, filled with
helium gas of nominal isotopic purity of 99.9999% at
a pressure of 142 mbar in static conditions.
10-um-thick deltaE and 500-um-thick
detector to measure the residual energy.
Two 200 um thick silicon surface barrier detectors
to measure elastic scattering from Au.
The thick target resonant scattering in inverse
kinematics
The properties of the F-19 states above and
below the p-emission threshold studied by R-matrix
analysis and presented in tables III and II
respectively.
Data received from Dr. Marco La Cognata.
VS
No information on sources of uncertainties.
Plotted as a function of the 19F excitation
energy on Fig. 2 of Phys.Rev.C99(2019)034301.
